title: Redis Data Integration Configuration File
description: Configuration file for Redis Data Integration (RDI) source collectors and target connections.

errorMessage.type: Configuration must be either an object or null

sources.title: Source collectors
sources.description: Source collectors that capture changes from upstream databases. Each key is a unique source identifier; the value configures one collector.

errorMessage.type: Sources configuration must be an object containing named source collectors

connection.title: Source database connection
connection.description: Connection configuration for a non-Redis source database. The exact set of properties depends on the database type.

SQL database.title: SQL database
SQL database.description: Connection configuration for a supported SQL database.

type.title: Database type
type.errorMessage: Database type must be one of: db2, mariadb, mysql, oracle, postgresql, sqlserver

host.title: Database host
host.description: Hostname or IP address of the SQL database server.
host.errorMessage: Host must be a valid hostname or IP address

port.title: Database port
port.description: Network port on which the SQL database server is listening.

errorMessage.oneOf: Port must be either a number between 1-65535 or an environment variable reference
errorMessage.pattern: Environment variable reference must be in ${VAR_NAME} format
errorMessage.minimum: Port number must be at least 1

database.title: Database name
database.description: Name of the database to connect to.
database.errorMessage: Database name must be a valid string

user.title: Database user
user.description: Username for authentication to the SQL database.
user.errorMessage: Database user must be a valid string

password.title: Database password
password.description: Password for authentication to the SQL database.
password.errorMessage: Database password must be a valid string

MongoDB.title: MongoDB
MongoDB.description: Connection configuration for a MongoDB database.

type.title: Database type
type.description: Database type identifier. Always `mongodb` for this connection.

connection_string.title: Connection string
connection_string.description: MongoDB connection URI including host, port, and any connection options.

database.title: MongoDB databases
database.description: Comma-separated list of MongoDB databases to monitor.

errorMessage.required: MongoDB connection string must be provided

Spanner.title: Spanner
Spanner.description: Connection configuration for a Google Cloud Spanner database.

type.title: Database type
type.description: Database type identifier. Always `spanner` for this connection.

project_id.title: Spanner project ID
project_id.description: Google Cloud project ID that hosts the Spanner instance.

instance_id.title: Spanner instance ID
instance_id.description: Spanner instance identifier within the project.

database_id.title: Spanner database ID
database_id.description: Spanner database identifier within the instance.

emulator_host.title: Spanner emulator host
emulator_host.description: Host and port of the Spanner emulator. Used for local development; leave unset against real Spanner.

use_credentials_file.title: Use credentials file
use_credentials_file.description: When `true`, RDI authenticates using a service account credentials file; when `false`, it uses application default credentials.
use_credentials_file.errorMessage: Use credentials file flag must be a boolean value (true/false)

change_streams.title: Change streams configuration
change_streams.description: Spanner change streams to capture, keyed by change stream name.

retention_period_hours.title: Change stream retention period hours
retention_period_hours.description: Retention period for the change stream, in hours.

errorMessage.minProperties: Change streams configuration cannot be empty

Snowflake.title: Snowflake
Snowflake.description: Connection configuration for a Snowflake database.

type.title: Database type
type.description: Database type identifier. Always `snowflake` for this connection.

url.title: JDBC URL
url.description: Snowflake JDBC connection URL, for example `jdbc:snowflake://account.snowflakecomputing.com/`.
url.errorMessage: URL must be a valid Snowflake JDBC connection string

user.title: Snowflake user
user.errorMessage: User must be a valid string

password.title: Snowflake password
password.description: Password for authentication to Snowflake. For key-pair authentication, omit this field and provide the private key via the `source-db-ssl` secret (`client.key` field).
password.errorMessage: Password must be a valid string

database.title: Snowflake database
database.description: Name of the Snowflake database to connect to.
database.errorMessage: Database name must be a valid string

warehouse.title: Snowflake warehouse
warehouse.description: Name of the Snowflake warehouse used for compute.
warehouse.errorMessage: Warehouse name must be a valid string

role.title: Snowflake role
role.description: Snowflake role used for the connection.
role.errorMessage: Role name must be a valid string

cdcDatabase.title: CDC database
cdcDatabase.description: Database hosting the CDC streams. Defaults to the main `database` if not set.
cdcDatabase.errorMessage: CDC database name must be a valid string

cdcSchema.title: CDC schema
cdcSchema.description: Schema hosting the CDC streams. Defaults to the main schema if not set.
cdcSchema.errorMessage: CDC schema name must be a valid string

errorMessage.required: Snowflake connection must include type, url, user, warehouse, and database.

name.title: Source name
name.description: Human-readable name for the source collector. Maximum 100 characters.

errorMessage.type: Source name must be a string
errorMessage.maxLength: Source name must be at most 100 characters

type.title: Collector type
type.description: Type of the source collector. Use `cdc` (default) for change data capture using [Debezium](https://debezium.io/). Use `flink` for Spanner change streams using the Apache Flink-based collector. Use `riotx` for Snowflake CDC using [RIOT-X](https://redis.github.io/riotx/).
type.errorMessage: Source collector type must be one of: cdc, flink, external, riotx

active.title: Collector enabled
active.description: When `true`, the collector runs; when `false`, the collector is disabled and produces no events.
active.errorMessage: Active flag must be a boolean value (true/false)

logging.title: Logging configuration
logging.description: Logging settings for this source collector.

level.title: Logging level
level.description: Log verbosity for the source collector.
level.errorMessage: Logging level must be one of: trace, debug, info, warn, error

errorMessage.additionalProperties: Unknown logging configuration properties. Only 'level' is allowed

tables.title: Tables to capture
tables.description: Tables to capture from the source database, keyed by table name. The value configures column selection and key handling for that table.

snapshot_sql.title: Snapshot SQL
snapshot_sql.description: Custom SQL statement used during the initial snapshot, giving fine-grained control over the data captured.
snapshot_sql.errorMessage: Snapshot SQL must be a valid SQL query string

columns.title: Columns to capture
columns.description: List of specific columns to capture for changes. If not specified, all columns will be captured. For RIOTX Snowflake sources, this is rendered as per-table `--table-columns` projection. Note: This property cannot be used for MongoDB connections
columns.errorMessage: Columns must be an array of column names

exclude_columns.title: Columns to exclude
exclude_columns.description: Specific columns to exclude from capture. When omitted, no columns are excluded. Only supported for MongoDB connections.
exclude_columns.errorMessage: Exclude columns must be an array of column names

keys.title: Message keys
keys.description: Optional list of columns to use as a composite unique identifier. For RIOTX Snowflake sources, this is rendered as per-table `--table-keys`. Only required when the table lacks a primary key or unique constraint. Must form a unique combination of fields
keys.errorMessage: Keys must be an array of column names that form a unique identifier

errorMessage.additionalProperties: Unknown table properties detected

schemas.title: Schema names
schemas.description: Schema names to capture from the source database. Maps to the underlying connector's `schema.include.list`.

errorMessage.type: Schemas must be an array of schema names
errorMessage.items: Each schema name must be a valid string

databases.title: Database names
databases.description: Database names to capture from the source database. Maps to the underlying connector's `database.include.list`.

errorMessage.type: Databases must be an array of database names
errorMessage.items: Each database name must be a valid string

advanced.title: Advanced configuration
advanced.description: Advanced configuration that overrides the underlying engine's defaults. Only required for non-standard tuning.

sink.title: RDI Collector stream writer configuration
sink.description:
Advanced configuration properties for the RDI Collector stream writer connection and behaviour. **Applies to the `cdc` and `flink` collector types.**

For the `cdc` collector type, see the full list of properties at [Debezium Server — Redis Stream sink](https://debezium.io/documentation/reference/stable/operations/debezium-server.html#_redis_stream). When using a property from that page, omit the `debezium.sink.` prefix.

**The properties listed below only apply to the `flink` collector type.**

redis.batch.size.title: Sink batch size
redis.batch.size.description: Maximum number of records the collector sink writes to Redis in a single batch.

redis.flush.interval.ms.title: Sink flush interval
redis.flush.interval.ms.description: Maximum time in milliseconds the collector sink waits to fill a batch before flushing it to Redis.

redis.connection.timeout.ms.title: Sink connection timeout
redis.connection.timeout.ms.description: Connection timeout in milliseconds for the target Redis client used by the collector sink.

redis.socket.timeout.ms.title: Sink socket timeout
redis.socket.timeout.ms.description: Socket read/write timeout in milliseconds for the target Redis client used by the collector sink.

redis.retry.max.attempts.title: Sink retry max attempts
redis.retry.max.attempts.description: Maximum number of retry attempts for failed Redis operations.

redis.retry.initial.delay.ms.title: Sink retry initial delay
redis.retry.initial.delay.ms.description: Initial delay in milliseconds before the first retry of a failed Redis operation.

redis.retry.max.delay.ms.title: Sink retry max delay
redis.retry.max.delay.ms.description: Maximum delay in milliseconds between retry attempts for failed Redis operations.

redis.retry.backoff.multiplier.title: Sink retry backoff multiplier
redis.retry.backoff.multiplier.description: Exponential backoff multiplier between retry attempts for failed Redis operations.

redis.oom.retry.initial.delay.ms.title: Sink OOM retry initial delay
redis.oom.retry.initial.delay.ms.description: Initial delay in milliseconds before the first retry after a Redis out-of-memory error.

redis.oom.retry.max.delay.ms.title: Sink OOM retry max delay
redis.oom.retry.max.delay.ms.description: Maximum delay in milliseconds between retry attempts after a Redis out-of-memory error.

redis.oom.retry.backoff.multiplier.title: Sink OOM retry backoff multiplier
redis.oom.retry.backoff.multiplier.description: Exponential backoff multiplier between retry attempts after a Redis out-of-memory error.

redis.wait.enabled.title: Sink replica wait enabled
redis.wait.enabled.description: When `true`, the collector verifies that each write has been replicated to the configured number of Redis replica shards before acknowledging it.

redis.wait.write.timeout.ms.title: Sink replica wait timeout
redis.wait.write.timeout.ms.description: Maximum time in milliseconds to wait for replica write acknowledgements.

redis.wait.retry.enabled.title: Sink replica wait retry enabled
redis.wait.retry.enabled.description: When `true`, the collector keeps retrying a write until replica acknowledgement succeeds; when `false`, it gives up after the first failure.

redis.wait.retry.delay.ms.title: Sink replica wait retry delay
redis.wait.retry.delay.ms.description: Delay in milliseconds between replica wait retry attempts.

errorMessage.additionalProperties: Sink configuration values must be strings, numbers, or booleans

source.title: Advanced source settings
source.description:
Advanced configuration properties for the source database connection and CDC behavior. **Applies to the `cdc` and `flink` collector types.**

For the `cdc` collector type, available properties depend on the source database — refer to the relevant Debezium connector documentation: [MySQL](https://debezium.io/documentation/reference/stable/connectors/mysql.html), [MariaDB](https://debezium.io/documentation/reference/stable/connectors/mariadb.html), [PostgreSQL](https://debezium.io/documentation/reference/stable/connectors/postgresql.html), [Oracle](https://debezium.io/documentation/reference/stable/connectors/oracle.html), [SQL Server](https://debezium.io/documentation/reference/stable/connectors/sqlserver.html), [Db2](https://debezium.io/documentation/reference/stable/connectors/db2.html), [MongoDB](https://debezium.io/documentation/reference/stable/connectors/mongodb.html). When using a property from those pages, omit the `debezium.source.` prefix.

**The named properties below cover the most commonly tuned settings: `spanner.*` properties apply to the `flink` collector type, all others apply to the `cdc` collector type. Any other property from the Debezium documentation can still be set as a free-form key-value pair.**

record.processing.threads.title: Record processing threads
record.processing.threads.description: Controls how many worker threads process captured records before they are written downstream.

snapshot.max.threads.title: Snapshot max threads
snapshot.max.threads.description: Sets the maximum number of threads used while taking the initial snapshot.

poll.interval.ms.title: Poll interval ms
poll.interval.ms.description: Defines how often the collector polls the source for new changes.

snapshot.fetch.size.title: Snapshot fetch size
snapshot.fetch.size.description: Defines how many rows are fetched per batch during the initial snapshot.

max.batch.size.title: Max batch size
max.batch.size.description: Caps how many records are processed together in a single batch.

max.queue.size.title: Max queue size
max.queue.size.description: Limits how many records can be buffered in memory before processing catches up.

heartbeat.interval.ms.title: Heartbeat interval ms
heartbeat.interval.ms.description: Sets how often heartbeat events are emitted to keep change tracking active. Use 0 to disable them.

heartbeat.action.query.title: Heartbeat action query
heartbeat.action.query.description: SQL query executed on the source whenever a heartbeat is emitted.

lob.enabled.title: Lob enabled
lob.enabled.description: Determines whether large object columns are included in change capture.

gtid.source.includes.title: GTID source includes
gtid.source.includes.description: Restricts MySQL GTID processing to the listed source UUIDs.

publication.autocreate.mode.title: Publication autocreate mode
publication.autocreate.mode.description: Controls whether and how the PostgreSQL publication is created or updated automatically.

publication.name.title: Publication name
publication.name.description: Sets the PostgreSQL logical replication publication name used by the collector.

slot.name.title: Slot name
slot.name.description: Sets the PostgreSQL replication slot name the collector reads from.

spanner.version.retention.period.hours.title: Spanner version retention period
spanner.version.retention.period.hours.description: Retention period in hours for Spanner change stream versions. Determines how far back the collector can resume after an outage.

spanner.fetch.timeout.ms.title: Spanner fetch timeout
spanner.fetch.timeout.ms.description: Timeout in milliseconds for a single change stream fetch request to Spanner.

spanner.fetch.heartbeat.ms.title: Spanner fetch heartbeat interval
spanner.fetch.heartbeat.ms.description: Interval in milliseconds at which Spanner sends heartbeat records when no data changes are available.

spanner.max.rows.per.partition.title: Spanner max rows per partition
spanner.max.rows.per.partition.description: Maximum number of rows the collector reads from a single Spanner change stream partition before yielding.

spanner.dialect.title: Spanner SQL dialect
spanner.dialect.description: SQL dialect of the Spanner database. Use `GOOGLESQL` for Google Standard SQL or `POSTGRESQL` for the PostgreSQL interface.

errorMessage.additionalProperties: Source configuration values must be strings, numbers, or booleans

quarkus.title: Quarkus runtime settings
quarkus.description: Advanced configuration properties for the Quarkus runtime that hosts Debezium Server. **Only applies to the `cdc` collector type.** See the [Debezium Server documentation](https://debezium.io/documentation/reference/stable/operations/debezium-server.html) for runtime configuration options. When using a property from that page, omit the `quarkus.` prefix.

errorMessage.additionalProperties: Quarkus configuration values must be strings, numbers, or booleans

flink.title: Advanced Flink settings
flink.description:
Advanced configuration properties forwarded to the Flink runtime that hosts the collector. Any property listed in the [Flink configuration documentation](https://nightlies.apache.org/flink/flink-docs-release-2.0/docs/deployment/config/) can be set here and will override the RDI default. **Only applies to the `flink` collector type.**

The properties listed below are the ones most likely to require adjustment. **Changing any other Flink property is not recommended unless instructed by Redis support.**

parallelism.default.title: Default parallelism
parallelism.default.description: Default parallelism for Flink jobs and operators. When unset, Flink uses the number of available task slots across all task managers (`taskManager.replicas × taskmanager.numberOfTaskSlots`). See [parallel execution](https://nightlies.apache.org/flink/flink-docs-release-2.0/docs/dev/datastream/execution/parallel/).

taskmanager.numberOfTaskSlots.title: Task slots per task manager
taskmanager.numberOfTaskSlots.description: Number of parallel task slots per task manager pod. Each slot can run one parallel pipeline instance, so this caps the parallelism a single task manager can absorb. See [task slots and resources](https://nightlies.apache.org/flink/flink-docs-release-2.0/docs/concepts/flink-architecture/#task-slots-and-resources).

taskmanager.memory.process.size.title: Task manager process memory
taskmanager.memory.process.size.description: Total memory budget for each task manager JVM process, expressed with a unit suffix such as `2048m` or `4g`. See [task manager memory configuration](https://nightlies.apache.org/flink/flink-docs-release-2.0/docs/deployment/memory/mem_setup_tm/).

errorMessage.additionalProperties: Flink configuration values must be strings, numbers, or booleans

resources.title: Collector resource settings
resources.description: Compute resources allocated to the collector. **Only applies to the `cdc` collector type.**

cpu.title: CPU resource value
cpu.description: CPU request for the collector container, for example `1` or `500m`.
cpu.errorMessage: CPU resource value must be a valid Kubernetes quantity string

memory.title: Memory resource value
memory.description: Memory request for the collector container, for example `1024Mi` or `2Gi`.
memory.errorMessage: Memory resource value must be a valid Kubernetes quantity string

errorMessage.additionalProperties: Unknown resources properties. Only 'cpu' and 'memory' are allowed

riotx.title: Advanced RIOT-X settings
riotx.description: Advanced configuration properties for the RIOT-X Snowflake collector. **Only applies to the `riotx` collector type.**

poll.title: Polling interval
poll.description: Interval between polls for new stream changes, for example `30s` or `PT30S`.
poll.errorMessage: Poll interval must be a valid duration string

snapshot.title: Snapshot mode
snapshot.description: Initial-load behavior. `INITIAL` performs a one-time snapshot before streaming; `NEVER` skips the snapshot.
snapshot.errorMessage: Snapshot mode must be either 'INITIAL' or 'NEVER'

streamPrefix.title: Redis stream key prefix
streamPrefix.description: Prefix used when constructing Redis stream keys, for example `data:`.
streamPrefix.errorMessage: Stream prefix must be a valid string

streamLimit.title: Maximum stream length
streamLimit.description: Maximum number of entries kept in each Redis stream before older entries are trimmed.
streamLimit.errorMessage: Stream limit must be a positive integer

keyColumns.title: Key columns
keyColumns.description: Deprecated RIOTX global fallback list of columns to use as message keys for every captured table. Prefer `tables.<schema.table>.keys`
keyColumns.errorMessage: Key columns must be an array of column names

clearOffset.title: Clear existing offset
clearOffset.description: When `true`, the stored offset is cleared on startup, forcing a fresh read.
clearOffset.errorMessage: Clear offset must be a boolean value

count.title: Record count limit
count.description: Maximum number of records to process. Set to `0` for unlimited.
count.errorMessage: Count must be a non-negative integer

errorMessage.additionalProperties: Unknown RIOTX configuration properties detected

java_options.title: Advanced Java options
java_options.description: These Java options will be passed to the command line command when launching the source collector. **Only applies to the `cdc` collector type.**

errorMessage.type: Advanced configuration must be an object

errorMessage.required: Source collector must include 'type' and 'connection' properties
errorMessage.additionalProperties: Unknown source collector properties detected

allOf.errorMessage: Schemas can only be defined for Oracle, PostgreSQL, SQL Server, Spanner, or Snowflake connections
Databases can only be defined for MariaDB, MySQL, or MongoDB connections
Snowflake does not support source-level 'databases'; use 'schemas' instead
Snowflake source requires 'schemas' at the source level
Cannot specify both 'database' in connection and 'databases' list - use one or the other
'columns' cannot be defined for MongoDB connections
'exclude_columns' can only be defined for MongoDB connections
RIOTX collector type requires a Snowflake connection
Snowflake connection type requires RIOTX collector type
Flink collector type requires a Spanner connection
Spanner connection type requires Flink collector type

targets.title: Target connections
targets.description: Target Redis databases where processed records are written. Each key is a target identifier; the value configures the connection.

connection.title: Database connection
connection.description: Connection configuration for a Redis database.

type.title: Database type
type.description: Database type identifier. Always `redis` for this connection.

host.title: Database host
host.description: Hostname or IP address of the Redis server.
host.errorMessage: Host must be a valid hostname or IP address

port.title: Database port
port.description: Network port on which the Redis server is listening.

errorMessage.oneOf: Port must be either a number between 1-65535 or an environment variable reference
errorMessage.pattern: Environment variable reference must be in ${VAR_NAME} format
errorMessage.minimum: Port number must be at least 1

user.title: Database user
user.description: Username for authentication to the Redis database.
user.errorMessage: Database user must be a valid string

password.title: Database password
password.description: Password for authentication to the Redis database.
password.errorMessage: Database password must be a valid string

key.title: Private key file
key.description: Path to the private key file used for SSL/TLS client authentication.
key.errorMessage: Private key file path must be a valid string

key_password.title: Private key password
key_password.description: Password used to decrypt the private key file.
key_password.errorMessage: Private key password must be a valid string

cert.title: Client certificate
cert.description: Path to the client certificate file used for SSL/TLS client authentication.
cert.errorMessage: Client certificate path must be a valid string

cacert.title: CA certificate
cacert.description: Path to the Certificate Authority (CA) certificate file used to verify the server's TLS certificate.
cacert.errorMessage: CA certificate path must be a valid string

errorMessage.required: Connection must include type, host, and port

dependentRequired.key: When using key authentication, both key and cert must be provided
dependentRequired.cert: When using certificate authentication, both key and cert must be provided
dependentRequired.key_password: Key password can only be specified when a key is provided

name.title: Target name
name.description: Human-readable name for the target connection. Maximum 100 characters.

errorMessage.type: Target name must be a string
errorMessage.maxLength: Target name must be at most 100 characters

errorMessage.required: Target must include a connection configuration
errorMessage.additionalProperties: Unknown target properties detected

oneOf.title: Database connection
oneOf.description: Connection configuration for a Redis database.

type.title: Database type
type.description: Database type identifier. Always `redis` for this connection.

host.title: Database host
host.description: Hostname or IP address of the Redis server.
host.errorMessage: Host must be a valid hostname or IP address

port.title: Database port
port.description: Network port on which the Redis server is listening.

errorMessage.oneOf: Port must be either a number between 1-65535 or an environment variable reference
errorMessage.pattern: Environment variable reference must be in ${VAR_NAME} format
errorMessage.minimum: Port number must be at least 1

user.title: Database user
user.description: Username for authentication to the Redis database.
user.errorMessage: Database user must be a valid string

password.title: Database password
password.description: Password for authentication to the Redis database.
password.errorMessage: Database password must be a valid string

key.title: Private key file
key.description: Path to the private key file used for SSL/TLS client authentication.
key.errorMessage: Private key file path must be a valid string

key_password.title: Private key password
key_password.description: Password used to decrypt the private key file.
key_password.errorMessage: Private key password must be a valid string

cert.title: Client certificate
cert.description: Path to the client certificate file used for SSL/TLS client authentication.
cert.errorMessage: Client certificate path must be a valid string

cacert.title: CA certificate
cacert.description: Path to the Certificate Authority (CA) certificate file used to verify the server's TLS certificate.
cacert.errorMessage: CA certificate path must be a valid string

errorMessage.required: Connection must include type, host, and port

dependentRequired.key: When using key authentication, both key and cert must be provided
dependentRequired.cert: When using certificate authentication, both key and cert must be provided
dependentRequired.key_password: Key password can only be specified when a key is provided

.*.errorMessage: Target must be either a Redis connection object or an object with a connection property

errorMessage.type: Targets configuration must be an object mapping target names to their connection settings

processors.title: Data processing configuration
processors.description: Settings that control how data is processed, including batch sizes, error handling, and performance tuning.

type.title: Processor type
type.description: Processor implementation to run. `classic` runs the classic processor; `flink` runs the Apache Flink-based processor (Kubernetes deployments only).

errorMessage.enum: Invalid processor type. The only supported types are 'classic' or 'flink'.

read_batch_size.title: Read batch size
read_batch_size.description: Maximum number of records read from the source streams in a single batch.

errorMessage.type: Read batch size must be either an integer or an environment variable reference
errorMessage.minimum: Read batch size must be at least 1
errorMessage.pattern: Invalid format in 'processors -> read_batch_size': ${VALUE} does not match the required pattern

read_batch_timeout_ms.title: Read batch timeout
read_batch_timeout_ms.description: Maximum time in milliseconds to wait for a batch to fill before processing it.

errorMessage.type: Read batch timeout must be an integer
errorMessage.minimum: Read batch timeout must be at least 1 millisecond

duration.title: Batch duration limit
duration.description: This property has no effect; use `read_batch_timeout_ms` instead.

errorMessage.type: Duration must be either an integer or an environment variable reference
errorMessage.minimum: Duration must be at least 1 millisecond
errorMessage.pattern: Invalid format in 'processors -> duration': ${VALUE} does not match the required pattern

write_batch_size.title: Write batch size
write_batch_size.description: Maximum number of records written to the target Redis database in a single batch.

errorMessage.type: Write batch size must be either an integer or an environment variable reference
errorMessage.minimum: Write batch size must be at least 1
errorMessage.pattern: Invalid format in 'processors -> write_batch_size': ${VALUE} does not match the required pattern

enable_async_processing.title: Enable async processing
enable_async_processing.description: When `true`, the processor handles batches asynchronously to improve throughput. **Classic processor only.**
enable_async_processing.errorMessage: Enable async processing flag must be a boolean value (true/false)

batch_queue_size.title: Batch queue size
batch_queue_size.description: Maximum number of batches queued for processing. **Classic processor only.**

errorMessage.type: Batch queue size must be an integer
errorMessage.minimum: Batch queue size must be at least 1

ack_queue_size.title: ACK queue size
ack_queue_size.description: Maximum number of batches queued for asynchronous acknowledgement. **Classic processor only.**

errorMessage.type: ACK queue size must be an integer
errorMessage.minimum: ACK queue size must be at least 1

dedup.title: Enable deduplication
dedup.description: When `true`, the processor deduplicates incoming records. **Classic processor only.**
dedup.errorMessage: Deduplication flag must be a boolean value (true/false)

dedup_max_size.title: Deduplication set size
dedup_max_size.description: Maximum number of entries kept in the deduplication set. **Classic processor only.**

errorMessage.type: Deduplication set size must be an integer
errorMessage.minimum: Deduplication set size must be at least 1

dedup_strategy.title: Deduplication strategy
dedup_strategy.description: This property has no effect — the only supported strategy is `ignore`. Remove it from the configuration. **Classic processor only.**

errorMessage.enum: Invalid deduplication strategy. The only supported strategies are 'reject' or 'ignore'.

error_handling.title: Error handling strategy
error_handling.description: Strategy for handling failed records. `ignore` silently drops them; `dlq` writes them to the dead-letter queue.
error_handling.errorMessage: error_handling must be 'ignore', 'dlq', or an environment variable reference

dlq_max_messages.title: DLQ message limit
dlq_max_messages.description: Maximum number of messages stored per dead-letter queue stream.

errorMessage.type: DLQ message limit must be either an integer or an environment variable reference
errorMessage.minimum: DLQ message limit must be at least 1
errorMessage.pattern: Invalid format in 'processors -> dlq_max_messages': ${VALUE} does not match the required pattern

target_data_type.title: Target Redis data type
target_data_type.description: Data type used to store target records in Redis. `hash` writes a Redis Hash; `json` writes a RedisJSON document and requires the RedisJSON module.
target_data_type.errorMessage: target_data_type must be 'hash', 'json', or an environment variable reference

json_update_strategy.title: JSON update strategy
json_update_strategy.description: Strategy for updating existing JSON documents in Redis. `replace` overwrites the entire document; `merge` merges incoming fields into it.
json_update_strategy.errorMessage: json_update_strategy must be 'replace', 'merge', or an environment variable reference

use_native_json_merge.title: Use native JSON merge from RedisJSON module
use_native_json_merge.description:
Controls whether JSON merge operations use the native `JSON.MERGE` command (when `true`) or Lua scripts (when `false`). Introduced in RDI 1.15.0. The native command provides 2x performance improvement but handles null values differently:

**Previous behavior (Lua merge)**: When merging `{"field1": "value1", "field2": "value2"}` with `{"field2": null, "field3": "value3"}`, the result was `{"field1": "value1", "field2": null, "field3": "value3"}` (null value is preserved).

**New behavior (JSON.MERGE)**: The same merge produces `{"field1": "value1", "field3": "value3"}` (null value removes the field, following [RFC 7396](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc7396)).

**Note**: The native `JSON.MERGE` command requires RedisJSON 2.6.0 or higher. If the target database has an older version of RedisJSON, RDI automatically falls back to Lua-based merge operations regardless of this setting.

**Impact**: If your application logic distinguishes between a field with a `null` value and a missing field, you may need to adjust your data handling. This follows the JSON Merge Patch RFC standard but differs from the previous Lua implementation. Set to `false` to revert to the previous Lua-based merge behavior if needed.

The Flink processor always uses the native `JSON.MERGE` command when the target database supports it. **Classic processor only.**
use_native_json_merge.errorMessage: Use native JSON merge flag must be a boolean value (true/false)

initial_sync_processes.title: Initial sync processes
initial_sync_processes.description: Number of parallel processes used to perform the initial data synchronization. For the Flink processor, parallelism is controlled by Flink properties instead. **Classic processor only.**

errorMessage.type: Initial sync processes must be either an integer or an environment variable reference
errorMessage.minimum: Initial sync processes must be at least 1
errorMessage.pattern: Invalid format in 'processors -> initial_sync_processes': ${VALUE} does not match the required pattern

idle_sleep_time_ms.title: Idle sleep interval
idle_sleep_time_ms.description: Time in milliseconds to sleep between processing batches when idle. **Classic processor only.**

errorMessage.type: Idle sleep time must be either an integer or an environment variable reference
errorMessage.minimum: Idle sleep time must be at least 1 millisecond
errorMessage.maximum: Idle sleep time cannot exceed 999999 milliseconds
errorMessage.pattern: Invalid format in 'processors -> idle_sleep_time_ms': ${VALUE} does not match the required pattern

idle_streams_check_interval_ms.title: Idle streams check interval
idle_streams_check_interval_ms.description: Time in milliseconds between checks for new streams when the processor is idle. For the Flink processor, use `processors.advanced.source.discovery.interval.ms` instead to configure a single discovery interval regardless of load. **Classic processor only.**

errorMessage.type: Idle streams check interval must be either an integer or an environment variable reference
errorMessage.minimum: Idle streams check interval must be at least 1 millisecond
errorMessage.maximum: Idle streams check interval cannot exceed 999999 milliseconds
errorMessage.pattern: Invalid format in 'processors -> idle_streams_check_interval_ms': ${VALUE} does not match the required pattern

busy_streams_check_interval_ms.title: Busy streams check interval
busy_streams_check_interval_ms.description: Time in milliseconds between checks for new streams when the processor is busy. For the Flink processor, use `processors.advanced.source.discovery.interval.ms` instead to configure a single discovery interval regardless of load. **Classic processor only.**

errorMessage.type: Busy streams check interval must be either an integer or an environment variable reference
errorMessage.minimum: Busy streams check interval must be at least 1 millisecond
errorMessage.maximum: Busy streams check interval cannot exceed 999999 milliseconds
errorMessage.pattern: Invalid format in 'processors -> busy_streams_check_interval_ms': ${VALUE} does not match the required pattern

retry_max_attempts.title: Maximum retry attempts
retry_max_attempts.description: Maximum number of attempts for a failed write to the target Redis database before giving up.

errorMessage.type: Maximum retry attempts must be either an integer or an environment variable reference
errorMessage.minimum: Maximum retry attempts must be at least 1
errorMessage.pattern: Invalid format in 'processors -> retry_max_attempts': ${VALUE} does not match the required pattern

retry_initial_delay_ms.title: Initial retry delay
retry_initial_delay_ms.description: Initial delay in milliseconds before the first retry of a failed write.

errorMessage.type: Initial retry delay must be either an integer or an environment variable reference
errorMessage.minimum: Initial retry delay must be at least 1 millisecond
errorMessage.maximum: Initial retry delay cannot exceed 999999 milliseconds
errorMessage.pattern: Invalid format in 'processors -> retry_initial_delay_ms': ${VALUE} does not match the required pattern

retry_max_delay_ms.title: Maximum retry delay
retry_max_delay_ms.description: Maximum delay in milliseconds between retry attempts.

errorMessage.type: Maximum retry delay must be either an integer or an environment variable reference
errorMessage.minimum: Maximum retry delay must be at least 1 millisecond
errorMessage.maximum: Maximum retry delay cannot exceed 999999 milliseconds
errorMessage.pattern: Invalid format in 'processors -> retry_max_delay_ms': ${VALUE} does not match the required pattern

wait_enabled.title: Enable replica wait
wait_enabled.description: When `true`, RDI verifies that each write has been replicated to the target database's replica shards before acknowledging it.
wait_enabled.errorMessage: Wait enabled flag must be a boolean value (true/false)

wait_timeout.title: Replica wait timeout
wait_timeout.description: Maximum time in milliseconds to wait for replica write verification on the target database.

errorMessage.type: Wait timeout must be either an integer or an environment variable reference
errorMessage.minimum: Wait timeout must be at least 1 millisecond
errorMessage.pattern: Invalid format in 'processors -> wait_timeout': ${VALUE} does not match the required pattern

retry_on_replica_failure.title: Retry on replica failure
retry_on_replica_failure.description: When `true`, RDI keeps retrying a write until replica replication is confirmed; when `false`, it gives up after the first failure.
retry_on_replica_failure.errorMessage: Retry on replica failure flag must be a boolean value (true/false)

on_failed_retry_interval.title: Retry interval on failure
on_failed_retry_interval.description: This property has no effect; remove it from the configuration.

errorMessage.type: Retry interval must be either an integer or an environment variable reference
errorMessage.minimum: Retry interval must be at least 1 second
errorMessage.pattern: Invalid format in 'processors -> on_failed_retry_interval': ${VALUE} does not match the required pattern

logging.title: Logging configuration
logging.description: Logging settings for the processor. **Flink processor only.**

level.title: Logging level
level.errorMessage: Logging level must be one of: trace, debug, info, warn, error

errorMessage.additionalProperties: Unknown logging configuration properties. Only 'level' is allowed

advanced.title: Advanced configuration
advanced.description: Advanced configuration for fine-tuning the processor. **All properties under `advanced` apply to the Flink processor only and are silently ignored by the classic processor.**

source.title: Advanced source settings
source.description: Advanced configuration properties for the source Redis client and streams reader. **Flink processor only.**

stream.name.pattern.title: Source stream name pattern
stream.name.pattern.description: Glob pattern used to discover input streams in the source Redis database, for example `data:*`.

discovery.interval.ms.title: Stream discovery interval
discovery.interval.ms.description: Time in milliseconds between checks for new input streams. Replaces the classic `processors.idle_streams_check_interval_ms` and `processors.busy_streams_check_interval_ms` properties.

batch.size.title: Source batch size
batch.size.description: Maximum number of records the source operator reads in a single batch. Alias for `processors.read_batch_size`; takes priority when both are set.

batch.timeout.ms.title: Source batch timeout
batch.timeout.ms.description: Maximum time in milliseconds to wait for a source batch to fill before processing. Alias for `processors.read_batch_timeout_ms`; takes priority when both are set.

connection.timeout.ms.title: Source connection timeout
connection.timeout.ms.description: Connection timeout in milliseconds for the source Redis client.

socket.timeout.ms.title: Source socket timeout
socket.timeout.ms.description: Socket read/write timeout in milliseconds for the source Redis client.

retry.max.attempts.title: Source retry max attempts
retry.max.attempts.description: Maximum number of retry attempts for failed source Redis operations.

retry.initial.delay.ms.title: Source retry initial delay
retry.initial.delay.ms.description: Initial delay in milliseconds before the first retry of a failed source Redis operation.

retry.max.delay.ms.title: Source retry max delay
retry.max.delay.ms.description: Maximum delay in milliseconds between retry attempts for source Redis operations.

retry.backoff.multiplier.title: Source retry backoff multiplier
retry.backoff.multiplier.description: Exponential backoff multiplier between retry attempts for source Redis operations.

errorMessage.additionalProperties: Source configuration values must be strings, numbers, or booleans

target.title: Advanced target settings
target.description: Advanced configuration properties for the target Redis client and sink. **Flink processor only.**

batch.size.title: Target sink batch size
batch.size.description: Maximum number of records the target sink writes in a single batch. Alias for `processors.write_batch_size`; takes priority when both are set.

flush.interval.ms.title: Target sink flush interval
flush.interval.ms.description: Maximum time in milliseconds the target sink waits to fill a batch before flushing it to Redis.

connection.timeout.ms.title: Target connection timeout
connection.timeout.ms.description: Connection timeout in milliseconds for the target Redis client.

socket.timeout.ms.title: Target socket timeout
socket.timeout.ms.description: Socket read/write timeout in milliseconds for the target Redis client.

retry.max.attempts.title: Target retry max attempts
retry.max.attempts.description: Maximum number of retry attempts for failed target Redis operations. Alias for `processors.retry_max_attempts`; takes priority when both are set.

retry.initial.delay.ms.title: Target retry initial delay
retry.initial.delay.ms.description: Initial delay in milliseconds before the first retry of a failed target Redis operation. Alias for `processors.retry_initial_delay_ms`; takes priority when both are set.

retry.max.delay.ms.title: Target retry max delay
retry.max.delay.ms.description: Maximum delay in milliseconds between retry attempts for target Redis operations. Alias for `processors.retry_max_delay_ms`; takes priority when both are set.

retry.backoff.multiplier.title: Target retry backoff multiplier
retry.backoff.multiplier.description: Exponential backoff multiplier between retry attempts for target Redis operations.

wait.enabled.title: Target replica wait enabled
wait.enabled.description: When `true`, RDI verifies that each write has been replicated to the target database's replica shards before acknowledging it. Alias for `processors.wait_enabled`; takes priority when both are set.

wait.write.timeout.ms.title: Target replica wait timeout
wait.write.timeout.ms.description: Maximum time in milliseconds to wait for target replica write verification. Alias for `processors.wait_timeout`; takes priority when both are set.

wait.retry.enabled.title: Target replica wait retry enabled
wait.retry.enabled.description: When `true`, RDI keeps retrying a target write until replica replication is confirmed; when `false`, it gives up after the first failure. Alias for `processors.retry_on_replica_failure`; takes priority when both are set. When enabled, the Flink processor retries indefinitely until the checkpoint timeout, unlike the classic processor which retries once.

wait.retry.delay.ms.title: Target replica wait retry delay
wait.retry.delay.ms.description: Delay in milliseconds between target replica wait retry attempts.

errorMessage.additionalProperties: Target configuration values must be strings, numbers, or booleans

dlq.title: Advanced DLQ settings
dlq.description: Advanced configuration properties for the DLQ Redis client and sink. **Flink processor only.**

max.len.title: DLQ sink max length
max.len.description: Maximum number of messages stored per dead letter queue stream. Alias for `processors.dlq_max_messages`; takes priority when both are set.

batch.size.title: DLQ sink batch size
batch.size.description: Maximum number of records the DLQ sink writes in a single batch.

flush.interval.ms.title: DLQ sink flush interval
flush.interval.ms.description: Maximum time in milliseconds the DLQ sink waits to fill a batch before flushing it to Redis.

connection.timeout.ms.title: DLQ connection timeout
connection.timeout.ms.description: Connection timeout in milliseconds for the DLQ Redis client.

socket.timeout.ms.title: DLQ socket timeout
socket.timeout.ms.description: Socket read/write timeout in milliseconds for the DLQ Redis client.

retry.max.attempts.title: DLQ retry max attempts
retry.max.attempts.description: Maximum number of retry attempts for failed DLQ Redis operations.

retry.initial.delay.ms.title: DLQ retry initial delay
retry.initial.delay.ms.description: Initial delay in milliseconds before the first retry of a failed DLQ Redis operation.

retry.max.delay.ms.title: DLQ retry max delay
retry.max.delay.ms.description: Maximum delay in milliseconds between retry attempts for DLQ Redis operations.

retry.backoff.multiplier.title: DLQ retry backoff multiplier
retry.backoff.multiplier.description: Exponential backoff multiplier between retry attempts for DLQ Redis operations.

wait.enabled.title: DLQ replica wait enabled
wait.enabled.description: When `true`, RDI verifies that each DLQ write has been replicated to the DLQ database's replica shards before acknowledging it.

wait.write.timeout.ms.title: DLQ replica wait timeout
wait.write.timeout.ms.description: Maximum time in milliseconds to wait for DLQ replica write verification.

wait.retry.enabled.title: DLQ replica wait retry enabled
wait.retry.enabled.description: When `true`, RDI keeps retrying a DLQ write until replica replication is confirmed; when `false`, it gives up after the first failure.

wait.retry.delay.ms.title: DLQ replica wait retry delay
wait.retry.delay.ms.description: Delay in milliseconds between DLQ replica wait retry attempts.

errorMessage.additionalProperties: DLQ configuration values must be strings, numbers, or booleans

processor.title: Advanced processor settings
processor.description: Advanced configuration properties for the processor. **Flink processor only.**

default.data.type.title: Default target data type
default.data.type.description: Data type to use in Redis when not overridden per job: `hash` for Redis Hash, `json` for RedisJSON. Alias for `processors.target_data_type`; takes priority when both are set.

default.json.update.strategy.title: Default JSON update strategy
default.json.update.strategy.description: Strategy for updating JSON data in Redis: `replace` to overwrite the entire JSON object, `merge` to merge new data with the existing JSON object. Alias for `processors.json_update_strategy`; takes priority when both are set.

dlq.enabled.title: Enable DLQ
dlq.enabled.description: When `true`, rejected messages are stored in the dead-letter queue; when `false`, errors are silently skipped. Alias for `processors.error_handling`; takes priority when both are set.

errorMessage.additionalProperties: Processor configuration values must be strings, numbers, or booleans

flink.title: Advanced Flink settings
flink.description:
Advanced configuration properties forwarded to the underlying Flink runtime. Any property listed in the [Flink configuration documentation](https://nightlies.apache.org/flink/flink-docs-release-2.0/docs/deployment/config/) can be set here and will override the RDI default. **Flink processor only.**

The properties listed below are the ones most likely to require adjustment. **Changing any other Flink property is not recommended unless instructed by Redis support.**

parallelism.default.title: Default parallelism
parallelism.default.description: Default parallelism for jobs and operators. When unset, Flink uses the number of available task slots across all task managers (`taskManager.replicas × taskmanager.numberOfTaskSlots`). Increase to fan out work across more task slots; see [parallel execution](https://nightlies.apache.org/flink/flink-docs-release-2.0/docs/dev/datastream/execution/parallel/).

taskmanager.numberOfTaskSlots.title: Task slots per task manager
taskmanager.numberOfTaskSlots.description: Number of parallel task slots per task manager pod. Each slot can run one parallel pipeline instance, so this caps the parallelism a single task manager can absorb. See [task slots and resources](https://nightlies.apache.org/flink/flink-docs-release-2.0/docs/concepts/flink-architecture/#task-slots-and-resources).

taskmanager.memory.process.size.title: Task manager process memory
taskmanager.memory.process.size.description: Total memory budget for each task manager JVM process (heap + managed + network + metaspace + JVM overhead), expressed with a unit suffix such as `2048m` or `4g`. See [task manager memory configuration](https://nightlies.apache.org/flink/flink-docs-release-2.0/docs/deployment/memory/mem_setup_tm/).

errorMessage.additionalProperties: Flink configuration values must be strings, numbers, or booleans

resources.title: Advanced resource settings
resources.description: Compute resources allocated to the Flink job, such as the number of task manager pods. **Flink processor only.**

taskManager.title: Task manager resource settings
taskManager.description: Resource settings for Flink task manager pods.

replicas.title: Task manager replicas
replicas.description: Number of Flink task manager pods to run.

errorMessage.type: Task manager replicas must be an integer
errorMessage.minimum: Task manager replicas must be at least 1

errorMessage.minProperties: Task manager configuration cannot be empty
errorMessage.additionalProperties: Unknown task manager properties detected
Unknown resources properties detected

errorMessage.type: Advanced configuration must be an object
Processors configuration must be either an object or null
errorMessage.additionalProperties: Unknown processor configuration property detected

secret-providers.title: Secret providers
secret-providers.description: External secret providers used to resolve `${...}` references in the configuration.

type.title: Provider type
type.description: Secret provider backend. `aws` uses AWS Secrets Manager; `vault` uses HashiCorp Vault.
type.errorMessage: Secret provider type must be either 'aws' or 'vault'

parameters.title: Provider parameters
parameters.description: Configuration parameters for the secret provider.

objects.title: Secrets objects array
objects.description: Secret objects to fetch from the provider.

errorMessage.type: Secrets objects must be an array
errorMessage.minItems: At least one secret object must be specified

errorMessage.additionalProperties: Unknown secret provider properties detected

errorMessage.type: Secret providers configuration must be an object

metadata.title: Pipeline metadata
metadata.description: Optional metadata describing this pipeline, such as a display name and description.

name.title: Pipeline name
name.description: Human-readable name for the pipeline. Maximum 100 characters.

errorMessage.type: Pipeline name must be a string
errorMessage.maxLength: Pipeline name must be at most 100 characters

description.title: Pipeline description
description.description: Free-form description of what the pipeline does. Maximum 500 characters.

errorMessage.type: Pipeline description must be a string
errorMessage.maxLength: Pipeline description must be at most 500 characters

revision.title: Pipeline revision
revision.description: Pipeline revision number. Must be a non-negative integer.

errorMessage.type: Pipeline revision must be an integer
errorMessage.minimum: Pipeline revision must be greater than or equal to 0

tags.title: Pipeline tags
tags.description: Array of pipeline tags. Each tag must be a string of up to 50 characters, containing only alphanumeric characters, dots, dashes, or underscores, and must start and end with an alphanumeric character. Tags must be unique.

errorMessage.type: Each tag must be a string
errorMessage.maxLength: Each tag must be at most 50 characters
errorMessage.pattern: Each tag must contain only alphanumeric characters, dots, dashes, or underscores, and must start and end with an alphanumeric character

errorMessage.type: Tags must be an array of strings
Metadata configuration must be an object
